title: silene mesatlantica subsp. quilesii
intro: Silene mesatlantica subsp. quilesii, also known as Quiles' catchfly, is a rare plant species of the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae) that is native to the Iberian Peninsula. The plant has been reported to possess medicinal properties due to its high concentration of flavonoids, saponins, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits associated with Silene mesatlantica subsp. quilesii include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help in the treatment of various conditions such as wounds, infections, and gastrointestinal disorders. Herbal preparations of Silene mesatlantica subsp. quilesii can be made into infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be used topically or orally to harness its medicinal properties.
